paper_title: Genetic analysis of the invasive alga Didymosphenia geminata in Southern Argentina: Evidence of a Pleistocene origin of local lineages

methods.description: Collection of D. geminata mats from various rivers in Argentinean Patagonia and Tierra del Fuego, followed by DNA extraction for genetic analysis.

steps.description: D. geminata mats were collected at eight rivers distributed along about 1600 km in western Argentinean Patagonia and Tierra del Fuego.
steps.searchable_summary: collect D. geminata mats from specified rivers

steps.description: Sixteen samples were selected based on presence, abundance and integrity of cell content, complexity, river bed coverage, and geographic origin.
steps.searchable_summary: select samples based on specific criteria

steps.description: Details on sampling procedures and DNA extraction methods have been described elsewhere.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DNA extraction on selected samples

methods.description: Optimization of PCR amplifications using DNA templates from whole benthic samples and isolated didymo cells, followed by sequencing.

steps.description: PCR amplifications were optimized using DNA templates obtained from both whole benthic samples and didymo cells isolated from the mats.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize PCR amplifications using DNA templates

steps.description: PCRs were made using oligonucleotides described in previous studies.
PCR products were analyzed by electrophoresis in agarose gels stained with Redgel and visualization under UV light.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze PCR products using agarose gel electrophoresis

steps.description: Three independent PCRs were purified using QIAquick PCR Purification Kit and re-analyzed by electrophoresis.
The purifications were checked by photometric analysis using a Nano spectrophotometer and finally sequenced by Sanger chemistry.
steps.searchable_summary: perform photometric analysis and sequence DNA

methods.description: Inference of Maximum Likelihood trees using RAxML-ng under evolutionary models inferred by jModelTest 2.

steps.description: Maximum Likelihood trees were inferred by the program RAxML-ng under evolutionary models inferred by jModelTest 2.
steps.searchable_summary: infer Maximum Likelihood trees using RAxML-ng

steps.description: Tree searches consisted in 20 initial Parsimony trees that were rearranged by tree bisection and reconnection.
Branch supports were calculated with the bootstrap routines implemented in RAxML-ng and TNT.

methods.description: Penalized Likelihood analyses using the chronos function from the R package ape to estimate divergence times.

steps.description: Penalized Likelihood analyses were performed with the chronos function from the R package ape.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Penalized Likelihood analyses using chronos

steps.description: The performance of different k and λ settings was explored by a cross-validation criterion.
steps.searchable_summary: explore k and λ settings using cross-validation
